title: Derris benthamii
intro: Derris benthamii, also known as Pithraj or Kundru, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions, such as malaria, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Derris benthamii include rotenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. Derris benthamii is commonly used in traditional medicine and is also incorporated into various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powder formulations, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Derris benthamii, also known as Tephrosia benthamii, is classified under the family Fabaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the legume family. Derris benthamii is further classified under the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Millettieae. The plant is native to Asia and is widely distributed throughout the tropical regions of the continent. Its classification is also aligned with the APG IV system, which groups it under the order Fabales.
uses: Derris benthamii, also known as the Pigeon pea, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and seeds are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds and infections. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Derris benthamii is also said to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Derris benthamii for medicinal uses.

properties: Derris benthamii, a plant species, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. constituents: Derris benthamii contains rotenone as one of its active constituents, which is a toxic compound used as a pesticide and insecticide. It also contains other compounds like desacetylarotenone, desacetylrotenone, and desacetylarotenol. The roots of D. benthamii are rich in rotenone, which is responsible for its toxic properties. Additionally, the plant contains a compound called 4'-hydroxyrotenone, which is a derivative of rotenone. The toxic properties of D. benthamii make it a potential candidate for use in pest control, but its use requires careful handling due to its high toxicity.
